title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Creamy, rich, and bursting with flavor – perfect for a delicious lunch!
lowCompetitionKeywords: restaurant style Butter Chicken at home
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani served with rice and fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, creamy taste of homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a classic Indian delight.

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian dish featuring tender chicken pieces cooked in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. Infused with aromatic spices and finished with butter and cream, it offers a delightful balance of tanginess and sweetness. This iconic curry is perfect for a comforting and satisfying lunch.

ingredients.item: Chicken breast or thigh, boneless, skinless

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chili powder, garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 15 minutes, or up to 2 hours for deeper flavor.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter and 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Sear the chicken for 3-4 minutes on each side until lightly browned and slightly cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another tablespoon of butter. Add the finely chopped onion, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and cloves. Saute until the onions turn translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Blend the Makhani Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree into the onion mixture. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides. Remove the whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, cloves). Transfer the sauce to a blender, add the soaked cashews (drained), and blend until completely smooth. You may add a splash of water if needed to achieve a smooth consistency.

instructions.title: Simmer the Curry
instructions.text: Pour the blended sauce back into the pan. Bring it to a gentle simmer. Add the seared chicken back into the sauce. Stir in the crushed Kasuri Methi and honey or sugar. Mix well and let it simmer on low heat for 10-15 minutes, allowing the chicken to cook through and absorb the flavors of the sauce. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter and the heavy cream. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, just until the sauce is heated through and creamy, but do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt if necessary. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving hot.

tips: For a truly authentic flavor, char the marinated chicken slightly in a tandoor or under a broiler before adding it to the sauce.
Always use good quality, ripe tomatoes for the puree; this forms the backbone of the sauce's flavor. For a richer color, you can add a pinch of Kashmiri red chili powder.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Serve with naan, roti, or basmati rice.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: Your Ultimate Guide to a Classic Indian Lunch Recipe

Craving a dish that’s rich, creamy, and bursting with flavour? Look no further than Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a culinary masterpiece that has captured hearts and palates worldwide. This iconic Indian lunch recipe is a testament to the country's vibrant food culture, offering a perfect balance of tanginess, sweetness, and mild spice. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cooking enthusiast, mastering this beloved dish is a rewarding experience that promises incredible taste and comfort.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, translates literally to 'chicken with butter.' Its origins trace back to the 1950s in Delhi, India, at the Moti Mahal restaurant, where it was created by Kundan Lal Jaggi and Kundan Lal Gujral. The story goes that they combined leftover tandoori chicken with a rich, tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter and cream, to prevent it from drying out. And thus, a legend was born.

The dish is characterized by its velvety smooth, bright orange or red gravy, which is primarily made from tomatoes, cashews, and a generous amount of butter and cream. Marinated chicken pieces, traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven), are then simmered in this luscious sauce. Unlike many spicier Indian curries, Butter Chicken offers a milder, sweeter, and incredibly fragrant profile, thanks to a blend of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), and cardamom. It's the ultimate comfort food, making it an incredibly appealing choice for a satisfying lunch or a special dinner. Its global appeal lies in its universally pleasing taste and luxurious texture.

## Ingredients You Need

To create authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, you'll need a combination of fresh ingredients and aromatic spices.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chilli powder (Kashmiri chilli powder for colour)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice

For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 medium fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, optional)
*   1/2 cup water (or chicken broth)
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus extra for garnish)
*   1 tablespoon sugar (adjust to taste)
*   1 teaspoon salt (adjust to taste)
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed between palms
*   Optional: 1/4 teaspoon cardamom powder

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making delicious Butter Chicken at home is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for a perfect meal:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavour and tenderness.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, or until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan (add a little more oil if needed), melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Build the Sauce:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh tomato puree) and the soaked cashews (if using). Add the sugar, salt, garam masala, and cardamom powder (if using). Mix well, bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavours to meld.
5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra velvety texture, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.
6.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pan over low heat. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the water (or broth) and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavours of the sauce.
7.  **Add Cream and Kasoori Methi:** Stir in the heavy cream and the crushed kasoori methi. Cook for just 2-3 minutes more, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil vigorously after adding the cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
8.  **Serve:** Garnish with a swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro, if desired.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is all about attention to detail.

*   **Marination Time is Key:** The longer you marinate the chicken, the more tender and flavourful it will be. Overnight marination is highly recommended.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes or fresh, ripe tomatoes. The quality of your tomatoes significantly impacts the final taste of the sauce.
*   **Blend Until Smooth:** Don't rush the blending process. A perfectly smooth sauce is a hallmark of authentic Murgh Makhani. Straining the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve after blending will eliminate any small bits and create that luxurious, silky texture.
*   **Don't Skip Kasoori Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken flavour. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their aromatic oils.
*   **Balance Sweet and Tangy:** The amount of sugar can be adjusted based on the sweetness of your tomatoes and your personal preference. A good Butter Chicken has a delicate balance.
*   **Finish with Freshness:** A final swirl of fresh cream and a pat of butter just before serving adds an extra layer of richness and visual appeal.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments. For a classic Indian lunch, serve it hot with:

*   **Naan Bread:** Garlic naan or plain naan is perfect for soaking up every last drop of the rich sauce.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Fluffy basmati rice, plain or jeera (cumin) rice, provides a comforting base.
*   **Roti or Paratha:** These Indian flatbreads are excellent alternatives to naan.
*   **Side Salad or Raita:** A simple green salad or a cooling cucumber raita (yogurt dip) can cut through the richness of the curry beautifully.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is divine, feel free to experiment and adapt it to your preferences or dietary needs.

*   **Vegetarian Butter Chicken (Paneer Makhani):** Substitute the chicken with an equal amount of paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or even mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas. Add the paneer or tofu towards the end of the cooking process, after the sauce is complete.
*   **Spice Level:** If you prefer a spicier curry, add a chopped green chilli along with the onions, or increase the amount of red chilli powder in the sauce.
*   **Lighter Version:** Reduce the amount of cream or substitute some of it with milk for a slightly lighter sauce. You can also skip the final pat of butter.
*   **Smoky Flavour:** For a subtle smoky flavour reminiscent of tandoor cooking, you can grill or broil your marinated chicken until lightly charred before adding it to the sauce.
*   **Nut-Free:** If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews. The sauce will still be delicious, perhaps slightly less rich. You can try adding a tablespoon of melon seeds or sunflower seeds for a similar thickening effect.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a meal; it's an experience. Its creamy texture, harmonious flavours, and comforting warmth make it a truly special dish. By following this guide, you can recreate this iconic Indian lunch recipe in your own kitchen and delight your family and friends with a taste of culinary heritage. Enjoy the journey of making and savouring this timeless classic!
